provenance: S-014 TK-002 (Foundry Vertical Slice Model). Extracts core component C-003 (socket registry) from prose into a machine-resolvable contract.
note: One record per K-### socket. Each record declares the module-agnostic contract only — capability + contract surface + the request/response/invariant contract — never the implementation. This artifact TRAVELS with the Sockets family. Instance binding rows (which module fills a socket here) live in the GPT_OS Project Source Registry and validate AGAINST this contract; they are not stored here. Recall (K-001) carries a derived mirror of the ID map, but this Git artifact is canonical and wins on disagreement.

K-001.capability: Derived, provenance-bearing memory/context retrieval over approved canon, plus capture-class storage for DB-native data. The Wiki remembers; the recall module recalls.

fields.id: Optional bare typed ID (e.g. K-003, P-010) to resolve to its current name, source, and provenance.

fields.source: Provenance pointer to the canonical file/path/note the value derives from.
fields.freshness: One of fresh | stale | offline | unknown, carrying an asOf timestamp.

invariants: Every recall answer exposes source (provenance) and freshness; an answer without both is not contract-conformant.
Recall is a derived mirror. When a recall answer disagrees with the Git-canonical registry, the registry wins and the mirror is stale.
A consumer reaches recall ONLY through the declared entrypoint. Reading the recall module's files or database directly is a reach-around and is not a valid connection through this socket.
